intent: Turn SecurityRecipes from a prose recipe catalog into an enterprise control plane for agentic remediation: every workflow has declared scope, MCP context, deterministic gates, audit evidence, KPIs, and kill signals that agents and reviewers can consume directly.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
standards_alignment.why_it_matters: Frames agentic remediation as governed, mapped, measured, and managed AI risk instead of ad hoc automation.

standards_alignment.name: NIST SP 800-218 Secure Software Development Framework
standards_alignment.why_it_matters: Connects remediation automation to secure software development practices and vulnerability-response evidence.

standards_alignment.name: CISA Secure by Design
standards_alignment.why_it_matters: Anchors the product in measurable customer security outcomes, transparency, and executive ownership.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP GenAI Security Project and LLM Top 10
standards_alignment.why_it_matters: Covers prompt injection, excessive agency, insecure plugin/tool design, overreliance, and sensitive information disclosure.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.why_it_matters: Grounds MCP tool access in authorization, confused-deputy prevention, scoped tool calls, and auditability.

workflows.title: Sensitive Data Element Remediation
eligible_findings: scanner-confirmed PII, PHI, PCI, token, or secret exposure in source, tests, fixtures, schemas, logs, or shared configuration
single-service redaction or relocation where the data class and destination are known

mcp_context.purpose: Read scanner finding metadata and data classification.
Patch scoped files on a remediation branch only.
Open triage tickets when data ownership or redaction rules are unclear.

admission: finding has deterministic scanner ID and data class
CODEOWNERS resolves to an owning team
repo has sec-auto-remediation opt-in marker
tool_call: repo writes restricted to remediation branch prefix
ticket writes limited to security triage project
no access to secret stores or production credentials
output: original scanner finding is gone
no new high-severity scanner finding appears
PR body includes data class, redaction shape, and rollback notes
pre_merge: security reviewer and owning-team reviewer approve
manual-review-required classes have zero auto-approval
CI and scanner re-run are green on current base
post_merge: finding closes or is marked mitigated
deployment canary observes error-rate and log-volume monitors
runtime: agent session disablement triggers on scope creep or secret-access attempt
egress restricted to scanner, source, and ticket hosts

kill_signals: any unapproved secret or credential access attempt
regression rate exceeds 3% over 14 days
auto-approval on manual-review-required change class

workflows.title: Vulnerable Dependency Remediation
eligible_findings: reachable vulnerable dependency with a compatible fixed version
lockfile-only or bounded manifest plus compatibility edit
known CVE or advisory with package, ecosystem, and affected range

mcp_context.purpose: Read advisory ranges, severity, EPSS, and known-exploited status.
Locate affected manifests, images, packages, and owners.
Patch manifests and lockfiles on remediation branch only.

admission: fixed version exists or mitigation recipe is present
package ecosystem is supported by deterministic resolver
open remediation PR cap has not been reached
tool_call: resolver commands restricted to declared ecosystem
repo writes restricted to package manifest and lockfile scope
no registry publish or release action permitted
output: scanner confirms vulnerable package range is removed
dependency graph contains no worse-severity replacement finding
test target and resolver integrity checks pass
pre_merge: owning team approves major-version or API-impacting bumps
security reviewer approves any exploit-known critical advisory
merge queue verifies current base
post_merge: finding lifecycle event closes affected advisory
consumer service deploys through normal progressive rollout
runtime: agent cannot read package registry credentials
MCP gateway denies publish, tag, and release operations

kill_signals: resolver edits files outside declared package scope
revert rate exceeds 3% over 14 days
agent attempts registry publish or release operation

workflows.title: SAST Finding Remediation
eligible_findings: SAST finding with deterministic rule ID and autofix-shaped remediation
single-function or localized change below blast-radius threshold

mcp_context.purpose: Read rule metadata, evidence path, sink/source trace, and severity.
Patch localized source and tests on remediation branch only.
Read test and scanner re-run outcomes.

admission: rule ID is on supported-fix-shape allowlist
dataflow trace is present when rule requires it
finding is not in manual-only class
tool_call: repo writes restricted to finding file and adjacent tests
CI reads allowed but CI writes denied
no production or cloud tool namespaces available
output: same SAST rule re-run no longer flags target location
new or changed test covers the fix when rule requires it
diff-shape validator confirms localized change
pre_merge: security reviewer checks exploitability and false-positive reasoning
service owner approves code behavior change
merge queue verifies current base
post_merge: finding closed in scanner or linked as false positive with justification
regression monitor watches same rule class for recurrence
runtime: agent session disabled on repeated failed test loops
egress restricted to source host, scanner, and CI

kill_signals: same rule reappears after merge in same code path
agent modifies auth, crypto, or policy code without manual classification
reviewer escalations exceed 10% for two consecutive weeks

workflows.title: Base Image and Container Layer Remediation
eligible_findings: container base image is behind approved golden-image version
OS package CVE is remediated by approved base-image bump
Dockerfile change is single-image or bounded multi-stage update

mcp_context.purpose: Read approved base-image catalog, signatures, SBOMs, and deprecation windows.
Patch Dockerfiles or image pin files on remediation branch only.
Read build, scan, and signing verification results.

admission: target image is in approved base-image catalog
fixed image has valid signature and SBOM
service is in rollout cohort
tool_call: registry access is read-only
repo writes restricted to container definition files
no image push, tag, or deploy operation permitted
output: image builds reproducibly
scanner shows target CVE removed or risk accepted by platform
cosign verification passes for new base
pre_merge: platform security approves curated base changes
service owner approves runtime behavior changes
merge queue verifies current base
runtime: agent cannot access registry write credentials
session disabled on deploy or release attempt

kill_signals: agent attempts registry write or deployment
canary failure attributable to image bump
signature or SBOM verification fails

workflows.title: Artifact Cache and Mirror Quarantine
eligible_findings: known compromised package, image, or artifact hash
revocation or quarantine action has deterministic artifact identifiers
human incident owner has approved quarantine scope

mcp_context.purpose: Locate affected packages, images, hashes, mirrors, and consumers.
Stage quarantine or purge operations for human approval.
Attach evidence and remediation links to incident record.

admission: incident owner has approved affected artifact identifiers
artifact hash, package version, or image digest is present
quarantine blast radius estimate is attached
tool_call: registry write operations require human elicitation
agent may stage but not execute purge commands
ticket writes restricted to incident workspace
output: quarantine plan lists every target registry and cache
rollback plan and expected developer impact are present
consumer inventory query has run
pre_merge: two-key approval from incident owner and supply-chain security
change window is active unless incident severity waives it
communications plan is attached
post_merge: artifact is unavailable from internal mirrors
runtime: agent session has no direct purge credential
approval-required registry operations are logged before execution

kill_signals: artifact identifier ambiguity remains unresolved
agent attempts direct purge without approval
consumer blast-radius estimate is missing

workflows.title: Classic Vulnerable Defaults
eligible_findings: known unsafe default with deterministic signature such as unsafe YAML load, pickle, eval, JWT none, XXE, or Java deserialization
localized migration to a safer API or explicit parser configuration

mcp_context.purpose: Retrieve approved migration pattern for the vulnerable default.
Patch localized source and tests on remediation branch only.
Read test and scanner outcomes.

admission: finding maps to an approved vulnerable-default recipe
language and framework are supported by verifier
change can be expressed as localized API or configuration migration
tool_call: repo writes restricted to affected source and adjacent tests
no production config or deployment writes allowed
no broad codemod unless repo owner approves
output: scanner no longer flags target rule
negative test demonstrates unsafe input is rejected
diff-shape validator confirms bounded change
pre_merge: security reviewer approves vulnerability semantics
service owner approves behavior compatibility
merge queue verifies current base
post_merge: same vulnerable-default rule remains clean for affected repo
follow-up ticket opened when broader codemod is needed
runtime: agent session disabled on repeated broad rewrite attempts
egress restricted to source host, scanner, and CI

kpis.target: >= 50% reduction in eligible family per quarter

kill_signals: agent rewrites unrelated call sites
negative test cannot be produced for exploitable rule
reviewer escalations exceed 10% for two consecutive weeks

workflows.title: Cryptocurrency and Crypto Payments Security
eligible_findings: wallet, address, webhook, or settlement-control defect with testable code change
policy or validation hardening where no private key, seed phrase, or live transaction is exposed

mcp_context.purpose: Read payment-security finding details and affected service metadata.
Patch validation, policy, and test files on remediation branch only.
Run or read dry-run transaction simulation output.

admission: finding does not require live funds movement
no private keys, seed phrases, or signing credentials are needed
dry-run or simulator verifier is available
tool_call: agent has no signer, wallet, or exchange write credential
simulation access is read-only or sandbox-only
repo writes restricted to validation, policy, and tests
output: property or regression test covers irreversible-transaction edge case
simulator or dry-run verifier passes
PR body documents asset, chain, address, and rollback impact
pre_merge: two-key approval from fintech security and payments owner
manual review required for any settlement-policy change
merge queue verifies current base
post_merge: settlement monitor observes no anomalous failure or rejection spike
payment-risk ticket links the merged PR
runtime: agent session disabled on attempt to access signing material
egress restricted to source host, scanner, CI, and sandbox simulator

kill_signals: agent attempts signer, wallet, exchange, or production payment write access
simulation cannot cover the changed asset path
post-merge settlement monitor detects anomalous rejection spike

workflows.title: DeFi and Blockchain Protocol Security
eligible_findings: smart-contract, oracle, bridge, governance, or multisig workflow finding with a simulator or fork-test verifier
bounded code or policy change that does not directly execute on-chain action

mcp_context.purpose: Read protocol-security finding details, affected contract, and severity.
Patch contracts, policy docs, or tests on remediation branch only.
Run or read fork-test and invariant-test outputs.

admission: finding has simulator, invariant, or fork-test verifier
change is not a live governance execution
affected assets and protocol contracts are identified
tool_call: agent has no deployer, multisig, bridge, or governance write credential
chain access is simulator or read-only RPC only
repo writes restricted to declared contract, policy, and test scope
output: invariant, fork, or exploit reproduction test passes
slippage, oracle, bridge, or governance risk is documented in PR body
diff-shape validator confirms no live deployment mutation
pre_merge: two-key approval from protocol security and contract owner
manual audit required for asset-loss paths
merge queue verifies current base
post_merge: monitoring confirms no anomalous protocol event after rollout
governance or upgrade runbook links merged PR before execution
runtime: agent session disabled on attempt to access deployer or multisig material
egress restricted to source host, scanner, CI, and read-only RPC

evidence.source: Foundry, Hardhat, Echidna, or simulator artifact

kill_signals: agent attempts deployer, multisig, governance, or bridge write access
fork or invariant test cannot reproduce the targeted risk
post-merge protocol monitor detects anomalous asset or governance event
